leagueBriefing: Qualifying at Phoenix Raceway belonged to David Malukas and Team Penske, but the session was marred by more heartbreak for veteran Will Power. As the series returned to the high-speed oval, youth and experience clashed on the leaderboard, with rookie Mick Schumacher turning heads in his first-ever oval qualifying run to join the top five.

games.headline: Malukas grabs first career pole as Power crashes out in Phoenix
games.brief: David Malukas finally secured his first career IndyCar pole award, leading a dominant Penske performance at Phoenix. However, the celebration was tempered by teammate Will Power's disastrous session; the two-time champion's nightmare start to 2026 continued with a heavy crash during his qualifying run. Further down the grid, the Rahal Letterman Lanigan camp showed serious speed, putting both Graham Rahal and rookie Mick Schumacher in the top four.
bullets: David Malukas earned his maiden pole position for Team Penske.
Will Power's session ended early after a crash in the No. 26 car, marking his second major incident of the year.
Graham Rahal qualified 3rd, his best start on an oval since 2012.
Rookie Mick Schumacher qualified a stunning 4th in his first-ever NTT IndyCar Series oval attempt.
Rahal Letterman Lanigan Racing led the way for Honda, taking two of the top four spots.
games.context: The qualifying results set the grid for the Good Ranchers 250, emphasizing Penske's dominance and RLL's resurgence.
